mission.title: Install and Configure Kong Gateway
mission.description: Kong Gateway is a cloud-native, scalable API gateway that provides high performance and extensibility through plugins. It is ideal for managing microservices and API traffic, offering features like load balancing, authentication, and advanced AI traffic capabilities. Key trade-offs include the need for proper configuration and potential complexity in managing plugins.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
Install the Kong CLI to manage the Kong Gateway installation and configuration.

```bash

brew install konghq/tap/kong

curl -Lo kong.tar.gz https://github.com/Kong/kong/releases/download/3.9.1/kong-3.9.1.linux.amd64.tar.gz && tar -xzf kong.tar.gz && sudo mv kong/bin/* /usr/local/bin/

```

> For macOS users using Homebrew.
> For Linux users.
commands: curl -Lo kong.tar.gz https://github.com/Kong/kong/releases/download/3.9.1/kong-3.9.1.linux.amd64.tar.gz && tar -xzf kong.tar.gz && sudo mv kong/bin/* /usr/local/bin/

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Create a Kubernetes cluster using gcloud with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create kong-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust region and node count as needed.

steps.title: Set Up kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure kubectl to use the new Kubernetes cluster's credentials.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials kong-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig file.

steps.title: Verify Nodes
steps.description:
Check that the nodes in the Kubernetes cluster are ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in the 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Install Kong Gateway
steps.description:
Install Kong Gateway using Helm with the specified version.

```bash

helm repo add kong https://charts.konghq.com

helm install kong kong/kong --version 3.9.1 --namespace kong --create-namespace

```

> Add the Kong Helm repository.
> Install Kong in the 'kong' namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Configure autoscaling and monitoring for the Kong Gateway.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Kong/kong/master/kubernetes/kong-ingress.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Kong/kong/master/kubernetes/kong-autoscaler.yaml

```

> This sets up the ingress resource.
> This sets up autoscaling.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kong Gateway
uninstall.description:
This will remove Kong Gateway and all associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

helm uninstall kong --namespace kong

gcloud container clusters delete kong-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> Uninstalls Kong from the specified namespace.
> Deletes the Kubernetes cluster.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kong Gateway
upgrade.description:
Backup your current configuration, drain nodes if necessary, perform the upgrade, and verify the installation.

```bash

kubectl get all -n kong -o yaml > kong-backup.yaml

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade kong kong/kong --version 3.9.1 --namespace kong

kubectl get pods -n kong

```

> Backup current configuration.
> Drain the node before upgrading.
> Upgrade to the specified version.
> Verify that all pods are running.
commands: kubectl get all -n kong -o yaml > kong-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kong ingress controller not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kong ingress controller may not be installed or running.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl get pods -n kong' to check the status of the pods.

troubleshooting.title: Error: '503 Service Unavailable'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Kong Gateway may not be properly configured or the upstream service is down.

**Fix:**
Check the configuration and ensure the upstream service is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to database'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Database connection settings may be incorrect.

**Fix:**
Verify the database connection settings in the Kong configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kong is not reachable'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Kong Gateway may not be exposed correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the service type is set to LoadBalancer or NodePort.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new plugins and improved AI traffic capabilities.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 3.8, review the new plugin configurations.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE' with all pods in the 'Running' state when everything is working.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding.
